finding: PHASE 1: the 4 support crates moved BYTE-FOR-BYTE via `git mv` (no source edit). `git diff` of every crate body across the move is rename-only; the sole Cargo.toml change is that `[lints] workspace = true` now resolves against sdk/Cargo.toml's new `[workspace.lints]` (mirrored 1:1 from src-app). Rust crate names unchanged → all `use sandbox_vm_protocol::/sandbox_seccomp::` in code_sandbox resolve unchanged, zero source edits.

PHASE 1 gates all green: `cargo check -p ziee`=0 (3 pre-existing unrelated warnings), `cargo check -p ziee-desktop`=0, `cd sdk && cargo check --workspace`=0 (stock, no DATABASE_URL). Confirmed each of the 4 crates checks explicitly (`cargo check -p sandbox-vm-protocol -p sandbox-seccomp -p sandbox-guest-agent -p sandbox-vm-launcher` Finished).

src-app/server/Cargo.toml repointed both consumed deps: sandbox-seccomp (optional, code_sandbox_seccomp feature, cfg(linux)) and sandbox-vm-protocol (cfg(any(linux,macos,windows))) → ../../sdk/crates/. The 4 crates' internal cross-refs (../sandbox-vm-protocol, ../sandbox-seccomp) stay valid: all 4 relocated together as siblings. src-app workspace members list drops the 4.

sdk is a git SUBMODULE (gitlink, mode 160000), not a subtree. The initial `git mv` crossed the submodule boundary and corrupted the top-level index (staged `D sdk` + renames INTO the submodule). Unwound via `git reset HEAD .`; verified sdk restored as a 160000 gitlink. Correct protocol: commit the crate moves INSIDE the sdk repo; the top-level ziee repo stages the src-app deletions + Cargo edits + the bumped sdk gitlink.

PHASE 2 STOP: the engine boundary on the CURRENT base is messier than the parked Increment-2 spec. The whole engine-candidate set's EXTERNAL crate:: surface is small (AppError, CodeSandboxConfig, get_app_data_dir, lit_search x2), but the parked 14-substitution list OMITS 5 real couplings: (A) lit_search /lit reverse-dep INSIDE build_bwrap_argv; (B) execute.rs→handlers::apply_workspace_mode; (C) mac_vm direct runtime_mount::READY read; (D) VM-backend embedded guest-agent staging (embedded::ensure/wsl2_agent_embedded/helper_service, uses get_app_data_dir); (E) streaming.rs progress-callback fetch + is_flavor_cached/is_fetch_in_flight. See BOUNDARY.md.

Hardening byte-identical audit is PENDING Phase-2 execution (nothing moved yet → no before→after argv to assert). The METHOD is preserved: for fixed (rootfs_mount_dir, CodeSandboxResourceLimits, ctx, command, caps), `assert_eq!(build_bwrap_argv(before), build_bwrap_argv(after))` (pure fn), plus empty `git diff` over the cgroup write_controller region + the SeccompPipe::install region. BLOCKER found by this audit: build_bwrap_argv currently contains a `crate::modules::lit_search` reverse-dep (the /lit --ro-bind, sandbox.rs ~L809-825) — the most security-sensitive function references an app module, so it cannot move to a build-DB-free crate until the /lit bind is lifted to a caller-injected extra_ro_bind (argv output stays byte-identical; builder SOURCE changes). No isolation weakened — the move is deferred, not faked.

PHASE 2 target: `grep -rn 'query!|query_as!|Repos|code_sandbox_versions|code_sandbox_settings' sdk/crates/ziee-sandbox/src` must be empty. Feasible: the engine's only DB-shaped coupling is via the two module globals (runtime_mount READY / runtime_fetch / resource_limits_cache CACHE) + version_manager's in-mem registry, all hoistable behind the injected RootfsProvider/ResourceLimitsProvider. CodeSandboxResourceLimits keeps `#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]` behind a macros-only sqlx dep (no DATABASE_URL) so ziee's query_as mapping survives. NOT yet created on this base (parked Inc-1 scaffold exists only in sdk-sandbox-wt).

The version_manager.rs split (91 KB) is feasible: ~10 DB sites (query!/query_as!/Repos) live in the registry region (L1-1370); the in-mem registry + fs helpers to move are localized in the tail (InflightKind@1374, InflightGuard@1382, register_mount@1427, acquire_inflight@1456, wipe@1816, sentinel@1853). DB half STAYS; the tail's TYPES move to seam vocabulary; ziee re-exports. Matches parked S-4. Real surgery on a large file, not a transcription.

Only linux_bwrap is runtime-verifiable here; mac_vm/wsl2 are #[cfg(target_os=macos/windows)] → NOT compiled on this Linux host. Boundary items C+D (READY global, embedded guest-agent staging) live on those arms, so a wrong seam-rewrite would NOT trip the Linux cargo-check gate but WOULD silently break the mac/windows builds (verified only on those hosts). Moving ~250 KB of under-specified, un-verifiable VM-backend code and claiming a pass is the specific risk that motivates the Phase-2 STOP.

PHASE 1: no handlers/OpenAPI touched → OpenAPI spec + types.ts unaffected (leaf-crate relocation only). PHASE 2 preserves this — every route/handler/model STAYS in ziee, so golden stays byte-identical both surfaces; to be confirmed at Phase-2 execution (regen, diff, `git checkout --`).
